The `sourceMappingURL` is a data URI containing a base64-encoded source map, which is common in transpiled or bundled code to facilitate debugging.\n\n---\n\n### Confirmed Findings:\n- **Presence of source map:** All reports correctly identify the embedded source map as a standard debugging aid, not malicious.\n- **No external input or untrusted data processing:** The code only performs static re-exports, with no dynamic or runtime data handling.\n- **No suspicious or malicious code:** No obfuscated logic, no code that reads or writes sensitive data, no network calls, no backdoors.\n- **Source map content:** The embedded source map is a normal JSON object with source info, not malicious code.\n\n---\n\n### Critical Review of Reports:\n\n**Strengths:**\n- All reports agree that the code is benign, with no malicious activity or security vulnerabilities.\n- The presence of source maps is acknowledged as standard practice, not an anomaly or threat.\n\n**Weaknesses or Overstatements:**\n- Some reports suggest that the embedded source map \"could hide malicious intent,\" which is an overstatement. Source maps are common and benign, though they can sometimes reveal internal structure if misused.\n- The scores assigned are mostly zero for malware and security risk, which aligns with the analysis. However, the confidence levels are high, which is appropriate given the simplicity of the code.\n\n---\n\n### Scores and Justifications:\n- **Malware score:** All reports correctly assign 0, which is appropriate.
